channel.description: World News Today: Read the latest world news headlines, Current International breaking news world wide. In depth analysis and top news headlines world wide.

image.title: World News, Today World News, Latest International News, World Breaking News, Trending News of World - Times of India

item.title: 'Fake News': Donald Trump on book that says he leaves leftover snack, silverware on bedroom floor
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Europe heatwave turns deadly as France records 1,000 excess deaths
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: 124 drones downed, civilians killed: Russia-Ukraine cross-border attacks escalate
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Watch: Joe Biden struggles to find stage exit after speech targeting Donald Trump
item.description: Former President Joe Biden delivered a strong speech attacking Donald Trump at a Maryland summit, criticizing his policies and calling them "vanity projects" and corrupt. The 83-year-old, who relied on a teleprompter and coughed during his remarks, also appeared to briefly search for the correct exit from the stage. This follows another recent on-stage moment where he seemed disoriented.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Millions drop Obamacare after Trump-backed subsidy rollback sends premiums soaring
item.description: Millions of Americans have lost their Obamacare health insurance as enhanced federal subsidies expired, causing premium costs to skyrocket. Over five million have dropped coverage, leading to a significant enrollment drop. While the Trump administration points to fraud, experts attribute the decline to unaffordable premiums, raising concerns about market stability and future insurance availability.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: The dark knight of Jalisco: Mexico's 'Batman' hunts motorcycle thieves, leaves them taped to lampposts
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Suit, tie and a splash: NYC mayor Zohran Mamdani revives pool tradition in full formal wear — video
item.description: New York City Mayor Zohran Mamdani has revived a decade-old tradition by taking a ceremonial plunge into a public pool, marking the start of the summer swimming season. Dressed in a full suit and tie, Mamdani's dive into the Thomas Jefferson Pool in East Harlem also celebrated 90 years of public swimming in the city, a legacy initiated by Mayor Fiorello LaGuardia.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Candace Owens questions Erika Kirk's 'vision from God' remark in resurfaced Charlie Kirk interview
item.description: Candace Owens reignited online debate after revisiting a past interview with the late Charlie Kirk, focusing on his wife Erika's suggestion that he keep his phone on the day of the assassination attempt. Owens questioned the unusual remark and implied it deserved closer scrutiny. Kirk had explained he witnessed the incident unfold from a gym before alerting his team that "something is very wrong." His account has resurfaced following his death.
item.dc:creator: Barsha Dutta

item.title: Nick White blasts Clavicular over viral kiss with trans model, says he destroyed the image that made him famous
item.description: Nick White criticized Clavicular after a viral video showed the influencer kissing Polish transgender model Adeli Pali, claiming the incident had severely damaged the image that made him popular. During his livestream, White argued Clavicular's brand would "never be the same" and compared the controversy to other influencers who lost credibility after public incidents. The clip has sparked widespread debate online, with opinions divided over its impact on Clavicular's reputation.
item.dc:creator: Barsha Dutta

item.title: Japan hit by 6.1-magnitude earthquake days after strong Iwate tremor, no tsunami threat
item.description: A magnitude 6.1 earthquake jolted Japan's Iwate Prefecture coast early Sunday, following a series of recent tremors. While no tsunami risk or immediate damage was reported, authorities are urging residents to remain vigilant due to the ongoing seismic activity. This latest quake occurred in an area previously hit by a stronger tremor, raising concerns about potential landslides as the typhoon season approaches.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Pakistan: 4 troops, 6 terrorists killed in 90-minute gun battle at Karachi rangers HQ
item.description: Pakistani security forces thwarted a major militant assault on a Sindh Rangers compound in Karachi, killing six terrorists and apprehending one. The coordinated attack, claimed by Jamaat-ul-Ahrar, resulted in the tragic loss of four Rangers personnel. This incident marks the first significant terrorist strike in the city since October 2024, highlighting ongoing security challenges.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: 'They came to take selfies, not to help': Venezuela quake victims rage as death toll hits 1,430
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: 'Vigilant, lethal and ready': US conducts strikes on 10 Iranian military targets — video
item.description: US forces launched strikes on 10 Iranian military targets near the Strait of Hormuz following an Iranian drone attack on a commercial oil tanker. The operation, ordered by President Trump, targeted surveillance, communication, and drone facilities. This escalation occurs despite an interim ceasefire, with Washington warning of further action if hostilities persist.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: 'Will be forced to militarily complete the job': Trump warns Iran after US strikes over 2nd ceasefire breach
item.description: The US launched fresh strikes on Iran after accusing Tehran of violating a ceasefire for a second consecutive day by attacking a commercial oil tanker near the Strait of Hormuz. This follows US retaliation for a drone attack on another vessel. Iran has warned of a "practical response" to the US actions, escalating regional tensions despite ongoing diplomatic efforts.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Indian student who works at Tesla says he faced F1 visa rejections twice: 'I stopped trying to win over, I built my profile'
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Nigerian proverb of the day: "One who has been bitten by a snake lives in fear of worms" and why old wounds make us fear harmless things
item.description: A Nigerian proverb, "One who has been bitten by a snake lives in fear of worms," highlights how past trauma can lead to unwarranted caution. While learning from painful experiences is vital for survival, the saying warns against letting fear expand to harmless situations. True wisdom lies in distinguishing real threats from imagined ones, preventing a life consumed by unnecessary dread.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Canadian proverb of the day: "Do not yell 'dinner' until your knife is in the loaf" and a lesson on humility, patience
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Quote of the day by US Supreme Court Justice Ruth Bader Ginsburg: "Fight for the things that you care about, but…"
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Venezuela jolted again: 5.6-magnitude earthquake strikes Aragua amid rescue efforts
item.description: Venezuela is grappling with a fresh 5.6 magnitude earthquake, compounding the devastation from earlier twin quakes that claimed over 1,430 lives. Rescue efforts are ongoing amidst widespread destruction, with thousands injured and homeless. International aid is arriving as authorities work to restore power and address critical shortages in the hardest-hit areas, while the UN estimates significant economic losses.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: This US county is offering $9,500 to live and work remotely from here. Condition is you have to stay for a year
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk
